info.title: TRACE Art Intelligence API
info.description:
AI-powered artwork identification, provenance cross-reference across 5 databases (Getty ULAN, GPI SPARQL, INTERPOL, ALR, AAMD, UNESCO), subscription management with Stripe, sacred geometry visualization, and real-time monitoring with self-healing capabilities.

## Authentication
- **Analyse API:** `x-api-key` header (auto-generated or user-configured via `ANALYSE_API_KEY`)
- **Ops/Agent API:** `x-api-key` header (via `OPS_API_KEY` or `ANALYSE_API_KEY`)
- **Auth (User):** `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header (from `/api/auth/login` or `/api/auth/register`)
- **Subscription:** `x-sub-token` header or `licenseKey` in request body
- **Tier:** `x-tier` header (`discover`, `collector`, `professional`)
- **CSRF:** `x-csrf-token` header for state-changing POST endpoints

tags.name: Health & Monitoring
tags.description: Server health, debug info, Prometheus metrics

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: Subscription management, Stripe checkout, license keys

tags.name: Provenance
tags.description: Artwork provenance cross-reference: Getty ULAN, GPI, INTERPOL, ALR, AAMD, UNESCO

tags.name: Ops & Agent
tags.description: Operations health checks, auto-fix patterns, watchdog reports

tags.name: Files
tags.description: App file listing and download for HQ admin

get.description: Returns server health status, API key configuration, Stripe status, provider health, disk space, memory, and active connection count.
Exposes all monitoring data in Prometheus text format: uptime, memory RSS, connections, SSE clients, errors, subscriptions, provider health, disk space, DB file sizes, credits.
Returns comprehensive server state: memory, config, subscriptions, rate limits, provider health monitoring, disk/SSL/credit status, memory trend, recent events and errors, DB file sizes.

Real-time SSE stream for operational alerts. Establishes a persistent connection that pushes provider degradation, disk warnings, SSL expiry, credit thresholds, and other system events. Heartbeat every 30s.

post.description: Proxies analysis requests to configured AI provider (Claude, Gemini, OpenRouter, or auto-fallback chain). Accepts Anthropic Messages API format with optional system prompt and image content.

model.description: AI model identifier (defaults to provider default)

200.description: AI analysis response in Anthropic Messages format

400.description: Invalid request (JSON parse error, missing messages, invalid content)

post.description: Creates a Stripe Checkout Session for subscription payment. Falls back to demo mode if Stripe is not configured.

200.description: Checkout session created or demo response

post.description: Receives Stripe webhook events (checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.deleted). Verifies webhook signature and auto-generates license keys on successful payment.
Admin endpoint to generate a license key manually. Requires adminToken matching ADMIN_SECRET on non-localhost requests.

200.description: License key created with subscription token

post.summary: Verify subscription token or license key
post.description: Verifies an HMAC-signed subscription token or a stored license key. Returns user tier, owner, and expiry on success.

get.description: Returns all active subscriptions with their license keys, tiers, owners, and expiry dates. Used by HQ admin panel.

post.description: Creates a new user account with email and password (PBKDF2-hashed). Returns a signed auth token and CSRF token. Rate-limited per IP (10/min).

201.description: User created — returns token, csrfToken, user info

400.description: Invalid email, weak password, or validation error

429.description: Too many registrations from this IP

post.description: Authenticates a user with email and password. Returns a signed auth token (7-day expiry) and CSRF token. Rate-limited per email (5 attempts/min).

200.description: Login successful — returns token, csrfToken, user info

post.description: Verifies an existing auth token. Returns user info if valid, or unauthenticated status if invalid/expired.

Full provenance cross-reference: Getty ULAN (artist name SPARQL), Getty Provenance Index (artwork SPARQL via CIDOC-CRM), INTERPOL Stolen Works Database, Art Loss Register (ALR), AAMD Nazi-Era Provenance Project, and UNESCO 1970 Convention. SPARQL queries run in parallel with synchronous mock checks.

post.summary: Search Getty ULAN artist names via SPARQL
post.description: Searches the Getty Union List of Artist Names (ULAN) public SPARQL endpoint. Returns artist names, birth/death years, nationality, and role. Falls back to mock data on error.
Builds a provenance knowledge graph from timeline data. Returns nodes and edges for visualization: creation events, ownership transfers, exhibitions, and auctions.

200.description: Knowledge graph with nodes and edges

post.description: Saves a provenance timeline with events, artist info, and metadata to the database.

400.description: Timeline title required or invalid request

get.description: Returns all saved provenance timelines, sorted by most recent first.

post.description: Deletes a saved timeline by title from the database.

400.description: Timeline title required or invalid request

get.description: Returns the last 50 telemetry events logged by clients.

post.description: Logs a client-side telemetry event (screen views, tier info, error messages). Sanitized for HTML and length-limited.
Legacy endpoint for cross-referencing artwork against INTERPOL, ALR, AAMD, and UNESCO databases. Uses deterministic mock data. New clients should use /api/provenance/cross-reference instead.

get.description: Exports scan results in CSV or JSON format by providing comma-separated IDs.
Lists all application files (HTML, JS, CSS, JSON, PNG, SVG) sorted by type. Used by the HQ admin panel for file download.
Downloads a specific application file by name. Path traversal is prevented by STATIC_DIR prefix check.
Returns detailed operations health status with issue detection, health score (0-100), and auto-fix suggestions.

200.description: Health check results with score and issues

get.description: Returns ops event log entries with optional type filtering.

post.description: Logs a new opertations event. Also broadcasts via SSE.
Matches an error message against known fix patterns and returns the suggested fix command/script. Does not execute by default — the caller decides whether to run the fix.

error.description: Error message to match against patterns

200.description: Pattern match result with fix details

get.description: Generates a daily operations report with health summary, top issues, and actionable recommendations.
Returns a detailed report from the AI self-healing agent: period, events today, errors, auto-fix success rate, provider health, top error sources, and recommendations.

post.description: Ingests watchdog batch reports from the client. Logs errors, matches against auto-fix patterns, and returns matched fixes.

Matches an error message against patterns and EXECUTES the fix command. Requires Ops API key. Fix commands are safety-filtered against shell injection (blocks `;`, `$(`, backticks). Sudo-requiring commands are reported but not executed.

error.description: Error message to match and fix

get.description: Returns the full OpenAPI 3.0 specification for the TRACE API.

ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyAuth.description: API key for /analyse endpoint. Set via ANALYSE_API_KEY env var or auto-generated.

OpsApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
OpsApiKeyAuth.description: API key for ops/agent endpoints. Set via OPS_API_KEY or ANALYSE_API_KEY env var.

CsrfToken.name: x-csrf-token
CsrfToken.description: CSRF token for state-changing POST endpoints. Obtained from login/register response headers.

SubscriptionToken.name: x-sub-token
SubscriptionToken.description: Subscription token for tier-based rate limiting.

TierHeader.name: x-tier
TierHeader.description: Subscription tier: discover, collector, or professional.
